title: Reconcile unrelated histories between SeniorDevBuddy local main and AgentForge canonical remote
author: Marcus Daley
objective: Detect and reconcile divergent main branches between a local feature/working repo (SeniorDevBuddy at https://github.com/GrizzwaldHouse/SeniorDevBuddy.git or local-only) and the canonical AgentForge scaffold remote (https://github.com/GrizzwaldHouse/AgentForge.git), preserving both histories via merge (--allow-unrelated-histories), surfacing conflicts for human decision rather than auto-resolving, and producing a clean push-ready main.

context.why_this_happened: Local SeniorDevBuddy was an unpublished working tree containing AgentForge experimentation, prototypes, extracted bundles, and the agentforge_autonomous/ TypeScript implementation. A separate canonical repo (https://github.com/GrizzwaldHouse/AgentForge.git) was initialized independently on 2026-05-03 with an architectural skeleton (contracts/, runtime/, schemas/, prompts/, docs/prd/, tasks/). When local main was first connected to the canonical remote on 2026-05-07, the two histories had no common ancestor.

constraints.rationale_for_no_rebase: Rebasing across unrelated roots replays each remote commit individually and forces hand-resolution per commit; a single merge commit with --no-ff produces a cleaner audit trail and preserves both DAGs intact.

detection_rules.unrelated_histories_indicator: git merge-base HEAD origin/main returns empty string AND exit code is non-zero
detection_rules.conflict_indicator: git status --porcelain shows entries with U[A-Z]|UU|AA|DD codes
detection_rules.stale_index_indicator: git status --porcelain shows entries that disappear after a no-op git reset HEAD
detection_rules.concurrent_worktree_indicator: git worktree list reports more than one worktree, especially under .claude/worktrees/agent-*

safe_fix_actions: Run git ls-remote against remote URL before any local config change to confirm reachability and probe HEAD
Use git remote add origin <URL> only after confirming origin does not already exist or already points elsewhere
Use git fetch origin with bounded timeout (120s)
Use git merge --allow-unrelated-histories --no-commit --no-ff for dry run; abort with git merge --abort before requesting decisions
Use git checkout --ours <file> or git checkout --theirs <file> only when policy says OURS or THEIRS for that specific file
For MANUAL or MANUAL_UNION resolutions, present the per-file diff and ask user to author the merged content; never invent merged content silently
After resolution, run git add <resolved-files> and git commit with the merge_message_template
Run git push -u origin main to set upstream tracking on first push
Never use --force or --force-with-lease without explicit authorization line in inputs
stop_conditions: Remote unreachable or returns non-zero from ls-remote
Existing origin points to a different URL than inputs.remote_url
Working tree has uncommitted changes that are not the merge in progress
Active worktree under .claude/worktrees/ has same branch checked out (would block branch operations)
Conflict count exceeds inputs.expected_conflicts AND no per_file_resolution_overrides exists for the new files
Any conflict marker (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) remains in any file after resolution
Push is rejected as non-fast-forward AND force authorization is not present
success_criteria: git merge-base HEAD origin/main returns a non-empty SHA after merge
git status reports working tree clean
Merge commit exists with two parents (local prior HEAD and origin/main HEAD)
Post-push: local main and origin/main point to the same SHA
All originally-tracked files from local main remain accessible on disk
All originally-tracked files from origin/main are now in the local working tree

reference_workflow_yaml.yaml_sketch:
name: Reconcile Split History
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      remote_url:
        description: 'Canonical remote URL to reconcile against'
        required: true
        default: 'https://github.com/GrizzwaldHouse/AgentForge.git'
      remote_branch:
        description: 'Remote branch to merge from'
        required: true
        default: 'main'
      conflict_policy:
        description: 'OURS | THEIRS | MANUAL | ASK_USER'
        required: true
        default: 'ASK_USER'
      authorize_push:
        description: 'Type ALLOW to authorize the final push'
        required: true
        default: 'DENY'

jobs:
  reconcile:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout local main
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Probe remote (read-only)
        run: git ls-remote ${{ inputs.remote_url }} ${{ inputs.remote_branch }}

      - name: Add canonical remote
        run: |
          git remote add canonical ${{ inputs.remote_url }} || git remote set-url canonical ${{ inputs.remote_url }}
          git fetch canonical ${{ inputs.remote_branch }}

      - name: Detect unrelated histories
        id: detect
        run: |
          BASE=$(git merge-base HEAD canonical/${{ inputs.remote_branch }} || echo '')
          echo "merge_base=$BASE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          if [ -z "$BASE" ]; then
            echo "histories_unrelated=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "histories_unrelated=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Dry-run merge
        if: steps.detect.outputs.histories_unrelated == 'true'
        run: |
          git config user.name 'agentforge-reconciler'
          git config user.email 'agentforge-reconciler@users.noreply.github.com'
          git merge --allow-unrelated-histories --no-commit --no-ff canonical/${{ inputs.remote_branch }} || true
          git status --porcelain | grep -E '^(UU|AA|DD|AU|UA|DU|UD)' > /tmp/conflicts.txt || true
          echo '--- Conflicts detected ---'
          cat /tmp/conflicts.txt
          git merge --abort || true

      - name: Open reconciliation PR
        if: inputs.authorize_push != 'ALLOW'
        run: |
          BRANCH=reconcile/split-history-$(date +%s)
          git checkout -b $BRANCH
          git merge --allow-unrelated-histories --no-ff -m 'Reconcile canonical scaffold (auto-merge, conflicts pending)' canonical/${{ inputs.remote_branch }} || true
          git push -u origin $BRANCH
          gh pr create --base main --head $BRANCH --title 'Reconcile split history with canonical scaffold' --body-file /tmp/conflicts.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Direct merge and push (only if explicitly authorized)
        if: inputs.authorize_push == 'ALLOW'
        run: |
          git merge --allow-unrelated-histories --no-ff -m 'Reconcile canonical scaffold' canonical/${{ inputs.remote_branch }}
          git push origin main

human_decisions_required.rationale: Both versions likely contain valid ignore rules; the union (deduped) is almost always correct.
human_decisions_required.action: Show user diff. Default action: produce union of both files, deduped, with section headers preserving origin attribution. User confirms or edits.

human_decisions_required.rationale: Local AGENTS.md (427 bytes) and canonical AGENTS.md may describe different agent rosters or different-generation systems. Cannot mechanically merge.
human_decisions_required.action: Show user both versions side-by-side; user picks one or authors a combined version.

human_decisions_required.rationale: Local README (17 KB, SeniorDevBuddy-focused) and canonical README likely describe different products. Loss-of-information risk if mechanical merge.
human_decisions_required.action: Show user both versions; user picks one or authors a combined README that covers the unified repo.

human_decisions_required.rationale: After merging, the unified repo will contain two product surfaces (SeniorDevBuddy orchestration + AgentForge canonical). Decide whether the unified repo lives at the AgentForge URL, at SeniorDevBuddy URL, or splits into a monorepo with subpackages.
human_decisions_required.action: User chooses one of: (a) keep AgentForge.git as canonical and push unified main there; (b) create a new SeniorDevBuddy.git and push there, leaving AgentForge.git as scaffold-only; (c) restructure into a monorepo and push only the agentforge_autonomous subpath into AgentForge.git via subtree.

remaining_risks: CRLF/LF normalization on Windows: git rewrites line endings on checkout, producing spurious 'modified' files in working tree after any merge or checkout. Cosmetic, but creates noise in git status.
Four parallel worktrees under .claude/worktrees/agent-* may race on .git/index. Recommend confirming all four are idle (or pruning them with git worktree prune) before reconciliation.
If decision D4 chooses option (c) monorepo split via git subtree, the strategy in this ticket does not cover it; a separate ticket is required for subtree extraction.
Conflict resolution for AGENTS.md and README.md may surface naming/identity differences between SeniorDevBuddy and AgentForge that warrant a project-rename or product-merge discussion separately.
Until D4 is decided, do NOT push the merge result anywhere — the destination remote depends on D4.
next_actions_for_executing_agent: 1. Read this ticket end to end before running any git command.
2. Confirm the constraints block has not been overridden by user override fields elsewhere.
3. Run the diagnostic_pipeline in order; halt on any stop_condition.
4. Surface the four human_decisions_required to the user in a single batch and wait for answers.
5. Only after D1-D4 are answered, perform the real merge with the user-supplied resolutions.
6. Run project health checks (npm install / npm run build / npm run lint in agentforge_autonomous/) post-merge before pushing.
7. Push only after authorize_push is explicitly granted by the user.
